denial_pattern_sources: plan_exclusion_overrides_state_mandate: NCSL State Health Insurance Mandates Database 2024; documented in CA, NJ, IL enforcement actions
missing_erisa_disclosures: DOL EBSA Technical Release 2011-01; common in Aetna pre-2024 templates
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 02/06/2026 for Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy related to Endometrial cancer, high-grade (C54.1). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG). The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

This determination applies to your employer-sponsored commercial benefit while you remain covered under an active group plan (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Endometrial cancer, high-grade (C54.1). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy recommended as the next appropriate step. Stage IB grade 3; LVSI present on pathology. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that missing peer to peer bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
